title: The Clockwork Cloud
summary: Lina races through Skyport to calm a runaway weather machine—and discovers that slowing down can be the fastest way forward.
purpose: Patience helps us solve hard problems.
body: High above the sleepy rooftops, Skyport's clouds were polished every morning until they shone like silver pillows. Lina loved watching the cloud-keepers work, especially when their brass weather machine clicked, hummed, and painted rainbows across the dawn.

But today the machine sneezed. It spun too fast, swallowed three clouds, and puffed out a fog shaped like a grumpy dragon. Inside the fog, the Mist chuckled. Lina grabbed her tool satchel and hurried toward the tower.

Every lever she pulled made the machine spin faster. Then Lina remembered her grandmother's advice: when a puzzle runs ahead of you, stop chasing and listen. She closed her eyes. Beneath the clatter she heard one tiny gear tapping out of rhythm.

Lina waited for the right moment, slipped the gear gently into place, and the tower gave a happy sigh. The dragon-fog dissolved into silver raindrops. Even the Mist had to admit that patience was a surprisingly powerful kind of magic.
That evening, Skyport's clouds glowed peach and gold. Lina wrote a new label for her tool satchel: Slow hands. Sharp eyes. Brave heart.
